source_jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT BATON

Baton equips business owners and buyers with a modern, data‑driven marketplace to buy or sell, offering valuations grounded in real financials and allowing them to close deals 2x faster than the industry norm. We recently https://finance.yahoo.com/news/baton-led-former-zillow-executive-174614569.html?guccounter=1 closed https://obvious.com/ideas/why-we-invested-in-baton/ our Series A to scale this vision further. We are proud to be building in person in New York City.




WHY JOIN US

 - Rapid momentum — We’re gaining traction quickly & our valuation tools, off‑market listings, and streamlined closing processes are changing how small business deals happen at scale.

 - Ownership from day one — As a small, lean team, every hire carries outsized impact; from shaping product direction to building a culture of customer-first execution.

 - In-person energy & collaboration — Based in NYC, we foster a tight-knit, energetic team environment where voice matters and decisions move fast.

 - Mission with heart — We’re clearing the path for small business owners to take meaningful steps forward by providing them clarity, speed, and confidence when it matters most.

 - World-class investors & runway for impact — Backed by Obvious http://obvious.com, Giant Ventures http://giant.vc, Divergent https://www.divergenthq.com, Trust Fund https://www.trustfund.vc, Burst Capital https://burst.vc, Bloomberg Beta http://www.bloombergbeta.com, and others, our Series A sets a deliberate course for growth and meaningful scale.

 - Competitive compensation & perks — Expect market‑competitive pay, equity, fully-covered health benefit options, flexible time off, commuter perks, in-office snacks, and a true culture of low ego and high respect.




THE BATON WAY

AI has unlocked a step change in what’s possible in this market — and in how Baton operates. Our goal isn’t to bolt AI onto existing workflows or launch a chatbot; it’s to re-architect how the product works, how internal operations run, and how value compounds across the platform. Engineers here use AI as a force multiplier in their own work and in what they ship — and we take the craft of production AI seriously.



How we build AI at Baton: We build agentic systems that handle real customer interactions and business workflows end-to-end. Expect to work across the full loop — from problem framing → data → evals → iteration → deployment → monitoring → continuous improvement; grounded in measurable outcomes (quality, latency, cost, reliability). We care about shipping quickly and building systems that hold up in the real world. We build in person in NYC because this work moves faster and gets better when the team is in the room together. We value low ego, high respect, direct communication, and real ownership.



Why join us: Baton has real momentum — we’re gaining traction quickly, and our valuation tools, off-market listings, and streamlined closing process are changing how small business deals happen at scale. Because we’re still a small, lean team, every hire has outsized impact: you’ll help shape product direction, build core systems end-to-end, and raise the bar on customer-first execution. And the work has real meaning — when we do our job well, we give small business owners clarity, speed, and confidence at one of the most important financial and personal moments of their lives.




ABOUT THE ROLE

As a Software Engineer, you’ll work directly with our Head of Engineering and product team to build the agentic platform that powers how small businesses are bought and sold. Your work will directly impact how thousands of owners navigate one of the biggest financial moments of their lives. This is not a papers-and-prototypes role, it’s product engineering with real AI systems. You’ll ship full-stack agentic systems end-to-end, collaborate closely with sales and operations, and turn real business needs into agentic product experiences that users interact with daily. AI isn’t a separate

source_jobs.title: M&A Advisor
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT BATON

Baton equips business owners and buyers with a modern, data‑driven marketplace to buy or sell, offering valuations grounded in real financials and allowing them to close deals 2x faster than the industry norm. We recently https://finance.yahoo.com/news/baton-led-former-zillow-executive-174614569.html?guccounter=1 closed https://obvious.com/ideas/why-we-invested-in-baton/ our Series A to scale this vision further. We are proud to be building in person in New York City.




WHY JOIN US

 - Rapid momentum — We’re gaining traction quickly & our valuation tools, off‑market listings, and streamlined closing processes are changing how small business deals happen at scale.

 - Ownership from day one — As a small, lean team, every hire carries outsized impact; from shaping product direction to building a culture of customer-first execution.

 - In-person energy & collaboration — Based in NYC, we foster a tight-knit, energetic team environment where voice matters and decisions move fast.

 - Mission with heart — We’re clearing the path for small business owners to take meaningful steps forward by providing them clarity, speed, and confidence when it matters most.

 - World-class investors & runway for impact — Backed by Obvious http://obvious.com, Giant Ventures http://giant.vc, Divergent https://www.divergenthq.com, Trust Fund https://www.trustfund.vc, Burst Capital https://burst.vc, Bloomberg Beta http://www.bloombergbeta.com, and others, our Series A sets a deliberate course for growth and meaningful scale.

 - Competitive compensation & perks — Expect market‑competitive pay, equity, fully-covered health benefit options, flexible time off, commuter perks, in-office snacks, and a true culture of low ego and high respect.




ABOUT THE ROLE

As an M&A Advisor, you’ll own and manage a book of deals that range in size from $400K to $10M+. You'll work with owners on listing strategy, offer negotiations, deal structuring, and diligence. Our advisors service small business acquisitions with the same level of sophistication as sell-side investment banks, while also providing a world class experience for owners who are likely going through their first ever exit. Lastly, as an advisor you will partner closely with our executive team, sales team, and product team to create winning and scalable systems that power Baton's growth.




ON A DAY-TO-DAY BASIS, YOU WILL...

 - Guide business owners through the selling process and advise on their listing strategy

 - Qualify and pitch buyers on Baton deals

 - Lead negotiations for dozens of business acquisitions

 - Manage diligence processes and navigate a complex lending landscape to ensure deals cross the finish line

 - Partner with the team to guide the product roadmap

 - Work to grow Baton and support millions of small business owners across the US
   
   


ABOUT YOU

 - You are passionate about helping people become successful small business owners and get paid for their life’s work

 - 2+ years of investment banking or M&A advisory or similar finance experience

 - 2+ years of professional experience at a high growth company

 - Experience interfacing with customers and managing complex interpersonal issues

 - Experience with financial modeling, Excel, and CRMs

 - You happily balance the high-level and low-level work; you wear many hats and are hands-on where needed to get things done; you pride yourself in being gritty

 - You fancy yourself an expert negotiator and are excited to continue learning




WHY BATON

 - Mission-driven work: empowering entrepreneurs and shaping the future of small business transitions.

 - A collaborative, data-driven, and growth-oriented culture.

 - An opportunity to make a significant impact at an early stage in our journey.


BENEFITS

 - Employer contribution for all healthcare options

 - 401k plan / short-term disability

 - Four weeks of paid time off, generous sick leave, and six weeks of flexible Work From Anywhere days

 - On-demand men

notes: Small job board with mostly engineering roles, recently closed Series A
